statement.markdown: Your task is to write a function that finds the product of all pairs of numbers in an array. The function should take as input an array of integers and return an array of products, where the product at index i is the product of all numbers at indices less than i. The function should also be optimized to handle large inputs efficiently. However, the current implementation has a bug that causes it to produce incorrect results.
statement.outputFormat: An array of integers representing the product of all pairs
statement.notes: Note that the product at index 0 should be 1, as there are no pairs to multiply

constraints.description: The length of the input array
The value of each element in the input array

examples.explanation: The product at index 0 is 1, as there are no pairs to multiply. The product at index 1 is 2, as it is the product of 2 and 1. The product at index 2 is 6, as it is the product of 2*3 and 1. And so on.

The product at index 0 is 1, as there are no pairs to multiply. The product at index 1 is 20, as it is the product of 20 and 1. The product at index 2 is 600, as it is the product of 20*30 and 1. And so on.

hints.text: Use the concept of prefix products to optimize the solution.

editorial.title: Optimized Pairwise Product Finder
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to calculate the prefix products of the input array, which can be done in linear time.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve calculating the product of all pairs for each element, which would result in a time complexity of O(n^2).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves calculating the prefix products of the input array in linear time, and then using these prefix products to calculate the final result.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the prefix products are calculated correctly, and then using these prefix products to calculate the final result.
commonMistakes: Failing to calculate prefix products correctly
Failing to use prefix products to calculate the final result
